title: Review Geographic Access Blocking — Non-NA Users Bypassing Restriction
description:
Users outside of the US and Canada were bypassing the geographic access restriction and getting into the system. One user from AP (Asia-Pacific) region gained access, and several users had no location recorded.

### Current Architecture
- All Salesforce-authenticated users are created in the database regardless of location
- A middleware checks user region on each authorized HTTP request
- Only US and NA locations are granted access to authorized pages
- Non-NA users are shown a region denied message

### Issue
The geographic check occurs at the middleware level after user creation, not at the authentication boundary. This allows user records to be created for non-NA users even though they cannot access the app.
acceptance_criteria: Non-NA users cannot bypass geographic access restrictions
All users have location data recorded during authentication
Geographic validation middleware correctly blocks non-NA access
middleware_changes: Reviewed geographic access middleware to ensure non-NA users are properly blocked. Location attribute checked during authentication flow.
implementation_notes: Architecture uses usedByTeam trait with middleware checking SalesforceInstanceLocationLookup for region. User records are still created in DB for non-NA users but access is denied at middleware level. Question raised about whether to block DB entry entirely based on location.
comment_summary: Waqar explained the current architecture where users are created in DB regardless of location, with middleware blocking non-NA access on each request. Asked whether DB entry should be blocked at the authentication boundary instead.
